appliedRows.title: [PDF] Online Sinkhorn: Optimal Transport distances from sample streams
appliedRows.text: Evidence quote: Online Sinkhorn: Optimal Transport distances from sample streams Arthur Mensch PSL University CNRS, ENS, DMA Paris, France arthur.mensch@m4x.org Selection reason: 权威学术会议论文，明确将Arthur Mensch列为作者，直接证明其与online_sinkhorn算法的研究关系。 Source preview: Online Sinkhorn: Optimal Transport distances from sample streams Arthur Mensch PSL University CNRS, ENS, DMA Paris, France arthur.mensch@m4x.org Gabriel Peyré PSL University CNRS, ENS, DMA Paris, France gabriel.peyre@ens.fr Abstract Optimal Transport (OT) distances are now routinely used as loss functions in ML tasks. Yet, computing OT distances between arbitrary (i.e. not necessarily discrete) probability distributions remains an open problem. This paper introduces a new online estimator of entropy-regularized OT distances between two such arbitrary distributions. It uses streams of samples from both distributions to iteratively enrich a non-parametric representation of the transportation ... MiMo rationale: 原始来源GitHub仓库信息密度低。候选中的NeurIPS论文是权威学术来源，明确将Arthur Mensch列为作者，直接证明其对online_sinkhorn算法的贡献，可作为补强来源。其他候选来源均未提及该项目。 Evidence requirements: Source must name Arthur Mensch directly. | Source must support why "online_sinkhorn" belongs on this person's page, or provide a better authoritative replacement. | Prefer official profile, institution/company page, paper detail page, author page, interview transcript, or reliable media with clear attribution. | Do not use search pages, login walls, empty captures, generic product pages without person role, or social snippets without context as replace_source. His research contributions have advanced AI through innovations in perception, reasoning, and decision-making under uncertainty. Dr. Horvitz is known for his contributions to AI theory and practice, with a focus on principl... appliedRows.title: [PDF] Sundar Pichai CEO, Google and Alphabet - Congress.gov
appliedRows.text: Evidence quote: Sundar Pichai CEO, Google and Alphabet Selection reason: 官方政府文件，标题和内容明确确认其CEO身份，来源权威且可访问，是强有力的直接证据。 Source preview: Sundar Pichai CEO, Google and Alphabet Sundar is the CEO of Google and Alphabet and serves on Alphabet’s Board of Directors. He joined Google in 2004 and helped lead the development of Google Toolbar and then Google Chrome, which grew to become the world’s most popular internet browser. In 2014 he was appointed to lead product and engineering for all of Google’s products and platforms - including popular products such as Search, Maps, Play, Android, Chrome, Gmail and Google Apps (now GSuite). Sundar became Google’s CEO in August 2015. He joined the Board of Directors of Alphabet, Google's parent company, in July 2017. Under his leadership as CEO, Google has been focused on developing produc... MiMo rationale: 原始来源（Interesting Engineering）文本预览为空，无法确认内容质量。候选来源中，World Bank Live和Congress.gov页面均明确、权威地证明了桑达尔·皮查伊作为Google和Alphabet CEO的身份，可直接替换原始来源。 Evidence requirements: Source must name 桑达尔·皮查伊 directly. | Source must support why "Sundar Pichai | CEO of Google and Alphabet | Bio - Interesting Engineering" belongs on this person's page, or provide a better authoritative replacement. | Prefer official profile, institution/company page, paper detail page, author page, interview transcript, or reliable media with clear attribution. | Do not use search pages, login walls, empty captures, generic product pages without person role, or social snippets without context as replace_source. appliedRows.title: Who is Sundar Pichai and what does Alphabet do?
appliedRows.text: Evidence quote: Sundar Pichai, the chief executive of Google, has been put in charge of its parent company Alphabet... Pichai's life story is remarkable, and his rise to the top of Google is an endorsement of India's standing in the global technology industry... Selection reason: 权威媒体BBC的专题报道，直接介绍桑达尔·皮查伊的生平、职位及成就，内容详实且可访问。 Source preview: Who is Sundar Pichai and what does Alphabet do? AFP Sundar Pichai Sundar Pichai, the chief executive of Google, has been put in charge of its parent company Alphabet, after co-founders Larry Page and Sergey Brin announced they were stepping down. The 47-year-old said the pair had set up a "strong foundation" on which he would "continue to build". Pichai's life story is remarkable, and his rise to the top of Google is an endorsement of India's standing in the global technology industry - and equally, a reassuring reminder of the so-called "American Dream". Pichai was born and schooled in Chennai, India. He captained his school's cricket team, leading it to win regional competitions. Getty Im... MiMo rationale: 原来源为儿童百科页面，AI相关性低。BBC报道权威、可访问，直接证明人物身份、职位及成就，适合作为替换来源。其他候选或权威性不足，或内容不直接匹配需求。 Evidence requirements: Source must name 桑达尔·皮查伊 directly. | Source must support why "Learn Sundar Pichai facts for kids" belongs on this person's page, or provide a better authoritative replacement. | Prefer official profile, institution/company page, paper detail page, author page, interview transcript, or reliable media with clear attribution. | Do not use search pages, login walls, empty captures, generic product pages without person role, or social snippets without context as replace_source. appliedRows.text: Evidence quote: Koray Kavukcuoglu, CTO, Google DeepMind and Chief AI Architect, Google Selection reason: 谷歌官方博客作者页，明确列出其职位为CTO, Google DeepMind and Chief AI Architect, Google，是权威的一手来源。 Source preview: The Keyword Koray Kavukcuoglu Facebook LinkedIn Mail ["What is the Fitbit Air?", "How can I learn new AI skills?", "What's the latest Android news?"] Subscribe Koray Kavukcuoglu, CTO, Google DeepMind and Chief AI Architect, Google Koray Kavukcuoglu CTO, Google DeepMind and Chief AI Architect, Google Koray is one of the world's foremost experts in artificial intelligence. As the CTO of Google DeepMind and Chief AI Architect at Google, he leads the development of Google DeepMind’s state-of-the art generative AI models and their integration into Google’s products at scale. Prior to his current role, he was VP of Research at Google DeepMind, where he founded the deep learning team and his team ... appliedRows.title: Why I Left Meta — Exit Interview With Mike Schroepfer
appliedRows.text: Evidence quote: Mike Schroepfer is Meta's former chief technology officer and currently a senior fellow at the company. He joins Big Technology Podcast for a look back at his time as CTO... Selection reason: YouTube视频标题和描述明确表明这是迈克·施罗普费尔本人参与的离职访谈，直接证明其离开Meta CTO职位的事实。 Source preview: Why I Left Meta — Exit Interview With Mike Schroepfer Alex Kantrowitz 58300 subscribers 20 likes Description 1320 views Posted: 8 Jun 2022 Mike Schroepfer is Meta's former chief technology officer and currently a senior fellow at the company. He joins Big Technology Podcast for a look back at his time as CTO: What went well? What could've been better? What changes would he have made in retrospect? Listen for an episode about the challenges of enabling massive scale, both from a technical and societal standpoint. 1 comments Transcript: we senior aka this ever excited interview shrek wrong his of handbook stay welcome thanks get lazy it pronounce uh definitely like years college around and in... MiMo rationale: 原始来源（路透社）因网页UI干扰导致质量存疑。候选来源中，维基百科页面提供了权威的职位任期信息，YouTube视频提供了本人参与的离职访谈，两者均可作为补强来源，直接支持其离职的核心事实。其他候选来源因缺乏直接证据或不符合要求被拒绝。 Evidence requirements: Source must name 迈克·施罗普费尔 directly. | Source must support why "Facebook's technology head Mike Schroepfer to step down" belongs on this person's page, or provide a better authoritative replacement. | Prefer official profile, institution/company page, paper detail page, author page, interview transcript, or reliable media with clear attribution. | Do not use search pages, login walls, empty captures, generic product pages without person role, or social snippets without context as replace_source. appliedRows.title: USC Alumni Paved Path for ChatGPT - USC Viterbi | School of Engineering
appliedRows.text: Evidence quote: The seminal paper “Attention Is All You Need,” which laid the foundation for ChatGPT and other generative AI systems, was co-authored by Ashish Vaswani, a PhD computer science graduate (’14) and Niki Parmar, a master’s in computer science graduate (’15). Selection reason: 权威机构页面，明确说明阿希什·瓦斯瓦尼是Transformer论文的共同作者，该技术是ChatGPT的基础。 Source preview: Informal Viterbi Logo USC Shield Logo USC Alumni Paved Path for ChatGPT From “eureka moment” to deployment, USC Viterbi alumni played a key role in the creation of ChatGPT (Left to right) Niki Parmar (’15) and Ashish Vaswani (’14) who pioneered the foundational transformer technology, and Barret Zoph (’16) tech lead at OpenAI. Photos/Vaswani and Zoph. (Left to right) Niki Parmar (’15) and Ashish Vaswani (’14) who pioneered the foundational transformer technology, and Barret Zoph (’16) tech lead at OpenAI. Photos/Vaswani and Zoph. ChatGPT has taken the world by storm, but seeds of the groundbreaking technology were sown at the USC Viterbi School of Engineering. The seminal paper “Attention I... Jakob started Inceptive with the goal of enabling a new generation of medicines reminiscent of software but running on our cells. Inceptive aims to accomplish this by learning life's languages with a unique combination of cutting-edge deep learning and novel, scalable biochemistry experiments. Before Inceptive, Jakob conducted research on deep learning at Google Brain, inc... appliedRows.title: The AI Pioneer Developing New … - What's Your Problem? - Apple Podcasts
appliedRows.text: Evidence quote: Jakob Uszkoreit is the CEO and co-founder of Inceptive, a biotech start-up. He’s also a co-author of “Attention is All You Need,” the paper that created transformer models. Selection reason: Apple Podcasts页面明确描述了雅各布·乌什科雷特作为Inceptive CEO、Transformer论文共同作者的身份，并讨论了其工作，是权威媒体来源。 Source preview: What's Your Problem? The AI Pioneer Developing New Kinds of Medicine Jakob Uszkoreit is the CEO and co-founder of Inceptive, a biotech start-up. He’s also a co-author of “Attention is All You Need,” the paper that created transformer models. Today, transformers power chatbots like ChatGPT and Claude. They’ve also led to breakthroughs in everything from generating images to predicting the structure of proteins. On today’s show, Jakob talks about the invention of transformer models. And he discusses how he’s using those models to try to invent new kinds of medicine, with a particular focus on RNA. See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information. Hosts & Guests Jacob Goldstein Jacob Goldst...
